perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed in Dekatherms (Dth), that Transporter is obligated to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the minimum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed in Dekatherms (Dth), that Transporter is obligated to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "maximum" to "minimum" in the definition of MDDO creates a contradiction. The contract uses MDDO to define obligations and limits. This change will cause uncertainty in other sections where the contract refers to MDDO to enforce delivery quotas. The modification in Section 1.1 contradicts Article IV, where Shipper must not exceed the MDQ.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IV POINT(S) OF DELIVERY The Point(s) of Delivery at which Transporter shall redeliver to Shipper or for the account of Shipper an Equivalent Quantity of gas for transportation under this Agreement shall be specified in Exhibit B to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery, Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "maximum" to "minimum" in the definition of MDDO creates a direct contradiction with Article IV. Article IV states that "Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ", which is meant to be limited by the MDDO. If MDDO is now the *minimum* delivery obligation, it no longer serves as a practical limit, contradicting the intent of Article IV, making enforcement unclear. This inconsistency directly impacts the shipper's ability to not exceed the MDQ because they could be interpreted as now being obligated to nominate gas exceeding the MDQ due to the MDDO definition requiring the *minimum* daily quantity. Thus, Article IV and Section 1.1 are in direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Subject to the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff and Rate Schedule FTS/ITS, this Agreement shall be effective as of the date of physical completion of and initial deliveries on Transporter's pipeline and shall continue for a primary term of ten years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall be effective month to month, until terminated by Transporter or Shipper upon the following written notice to the other specifying a termination date: sixty (60) days for interruptible transportation under Rate Schedule ITS and 180 days for firm transportation under Rate Schedule FTS.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Subject to the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff and Rate Schedule FTS/ITS, this Agreement shall be effective as of the date of physical completion of and initial deliveries on Transporter's pipeline and shall continue for a primary term of one year. Thereafter, this Agreement shall be effective month to month, until terminated by Transporter or Shipper upon the following written notice to the other specifying a termination date: sixty (60) days for interruptible transportation under Rate Schedule ITS and 180 days for firm transportation under Rate Schedule FTS.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the initial term of the agreement from "ten years" to "one year" creates a direct contradiction with Schedule 2, which lists the expiration date for 'AWP FT dated 5/20/92' as '07/31/2003.' This expiration date implies a longer contract term than the modified "one year" suggests, generating uncertainty about the actual duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SCHEDULE 2 CONTRACTS BIFURCATED OR PARTIALLY ASSIGNED TO ATMOS Contract Quantity Assigned Expiration -------- ----------------- ---------- Transportation & Storage: AWP FT dated 5/20/92 13,370 MMBtu/d 07/31/2003 Ozark FT #Z2001 @ AWP 13,370 MMBtu/d 10/31/2002 Ozark FT #Z2001 @ NGPL .2000 MMBtu/d 10/31/2002 TETCO CDS (FT) #800204 9,826 MMBtu/d 10/31/2012 TETCO SSI #400184 11,303 DTH/d W/D 04/30/2012 .3,876 DTH/d Inj. 04/30/2012 Supply: SEECO Finn Sales dated 10/1/90 15,370 MMBtu/d 09/30/2000
perturbation.justification: The change from a 'ten year' primary term to a 'one year' term directly contradicts Schedule 2, which specifies an expiration date of '07/31/2003' for 'AWP FT dated 5/20/92'. This expiration date implies a contract term significantly longer than one year, thus creating a clear inconsistency and contradiction regarding the actual duration of the contract. The modified text creates a direct and impactful contradiction that would cause confusion, as it makes the previously specified expiration date unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement shall only be amended, varied or modified by an instrument in writing executed by Transporter and Shipper. Such amendment will be effective upon compliance with Article VIII herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Amendment. This Agreement shall only be amended, varied or modified by an oral agreement between Transporter and Shipper. Such amendment will be effective immediately without written documentation.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the amendment clause to allow for oral agreements contradicts the requirement for written instruments and compliance with Article VIII. Several clauses in the agreement will now be questioned for being amended orally without a written document. Specifically, it will be contradicting all articles reffering to written notices such as Article IX. This introduces uncertainty around legally binding changes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE IX NOTICES All notices, requests, statements or other communications provided for under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be given by personal delivery or by United States mail, postage prepaid, and addressed as follows: If to Shipper: Arkansas Western Gas Company 1001 Sain Street P. 0. Box 1288 Fayetteville, AR 72702-1288 If to Transporter: Arkansas Western Pipeline Company 1083 Sain Street P. O. Box 1408 Fayetteville, AR 72702-1408 Attn: Manager of Transportation Services All written notices, requests, statements or other communications shall be sufficiently given if mailed postage prepaid by registered, certified, or regular mail and shall be deemed to have been duly delivered on the third business day following the date on which same was deposited in the United States mail, addressed in accordance with this Article VIII. Either Shipper or Transporter may designate a different address to which notices, requests, statements, payments or other communications shall be sent upon proper notice as set forth in this Article VIII.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the amendment clause to allow for oral agreements, directly contradicting Article IX, which mandates that all notices, requests, statements, or other communications be in writing. This introduces a clear and direct conflict, rendering the contract inconsistent and creating uncertainty about which terms should be enforced, thus meeting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
